chapter_title.ar: باب مَا لَا يَلْزَمُ مِنَ الْأَيْمَانِ وَ النُّذُورِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 7 – What does not necessitate, from the oaths and the vows

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/317)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/317)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ عَلَيَّ نَذْرٌ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ شَيْئاً قَالَ لَيْسَ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Sa’alba Bin Maymoun, from Ma’mar Bin Umar who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the man saying, ‘Upon me is a vow’, and he did not specify anything. He<sup>asws</sup> said; ‘It is not with anything’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Tha‘labah ibn Maymun from Mu’ammar ibn ‘Umar who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who says there is a vow on him but does not specify anything. He (the Imam) said, ‘He does not owe anything.’”
